footer.headline: A simpler waiting-room stack for clinics that need order now

landing.title: Clinic flow suite | kiosk + display + web
landing.description: Operations software for small and medium clinics with kiosk, waiting room display, reception console, and web booking connected together.
landing.heading: Clinic flow suite with kiosk, waiting room display, reception, and web booking

hero.title: Reception gets order now, patients stay oriented, and management gets numbers to decide.
hero.deck: The suite connects kiosk, calling display, operating console, and web booking under one state model. It rolls out fast, cuts repeated questions, and turns daily operations into something a buyer can actually read.

metrics.label: understands arrival and waiting
metrics.detail: QR, kiosk, and queue status tell the same story.

metrics.label: measures without asking for another report
metrics.detail: Wait time, no-show, and room load in one readable view.

panels.title: Call, reprint, and close without getting lost
panels.copy: Daily operations designed for real consult rooms and non-technical teams.

panels.title: Check-in and queue visibility from QR or WhatsApp
panels.copy: Patients arrive more oriented and ask fewer repeated questions.

panels.title: Clear live calls with privacy built in
panels.copy: Ticket code, initials, and consult room on a display the room can actually follow.

panels.title: Wait time, no-show, and consult room load
panels.copy: The daily experience ends in indicators that actually help a buyer decide.

focus.title: Three readings of the same product so the sale is easier to explain.
focus.deck: You do not need to promise a different system to every person. The same base solves daily operations, improves the patient experience, and leaves clear numbers behind.

cards.title: Operate without changing context
cards.copy: Everything critical happens on the same state model: arrivals, calls, reprints, closures, and traceability.
items: Fewer mental steps during peak hours
Shortcuts and control by room or station
A clearer console for active front desk work

cards.title: Understand arrival and waiting
cards.copy: Patients confirm whether they are scheduled, track their turn, and know what to do without depending on the counter.
items: QR, kiosk, and phone on the same queue
Fewer repeated questions in the room
More clarity before entering the consult room

cards.title: See why the suite is worth renewing
cards.copy: Operations leave useful trace data behind: wait time, no-show, and room load for decisions with context.
items: A dashboard ready for owner or manager
Reading by site, time window, and consult room
A cleaner argument for expanding the suite

modules.title: Start with the panel that gets used the most: reception and room coordination.
modules.deck: The sale becomes concrete when reception gains immediate control. Kiosk, display, and web booking stop acting like separate pieces and start supporting the same job.

cards.title: Queue operations for real consult rooms
cards.copy: Call, reprint, complete, flag no-show, and move the room from a console built for active front desk teams.

cards.title: Guided in-room check-in
cards.copy: For scheduled and walk-in patients, with quick help, large text mode, and visible support when reception needs to step in.

cards.title: Privacy-aware live calling
cards.copy: The screen shows ticket, initials, and consult room so the room understands what is happening without exposing sensitive data.

cards.title: Web booking and reminders connected to the queue
cards.copy: The website stops being a separate brochure and becomes the digital entry point of the same clinic flow.

journeys.eyebrow: Three readings of the same flow
journeys.title: The same operating layer answers different questions depending on who is looking.
journeys.deck: That lowers commercial friction: reception understands daily use, patients feel the order, and management sees the result.

lanes.title: Coordinate arrivals, calls, and closures from the same state
steps: Receives patients from kiosk, web booking, or mobile check-in without rebuilding the case.
Calls, reprints, completes, and flags no-show with visible traceability.
Leaves the data behind that later feeds the site dashboard.

lanes.title: Confirm arrival, follow progress, and know where to go
steps: Receives a WhatsApp or SMS reminder and enters from the link or QR.
If they still arrive in-room, the kiosk recognizes the same flow and avoids repeated questions.
Private queue status lowers anxiety while they wait.

lanes.title: Read wait time, no-show, and load without asking for another report
steps: The dashboard summarizes average wait, served tickets, and no-show.
The site sees bottlenecks by time window or consult room.
The conversation moves from intuition to measurable improvement.

surfaces.title: The landing sells the promise in the right order.
surfaces.deck: First the operating layer becomes clear. Then the patient experience becomes tangible. Finally the dashboard helps close budget conversations.

cards.title: Patients confirm from QR or direct link
cards.copy: This is the first tangible proof that arrival can happen without putting more pressure on reception.

integrations.title: The integration story should read just as clearly
integrations.deck: Buyers need to understand in minutes what enters the pilot, what connects on day one, and what can scale later without rebuilding the base.

pricing.title: Clear packages that sell value before complexity
pricing.deck: The decision should feel simple: start with waiting-room order, add patient visibility, and close with management insight.

plans.name: Lite Multi-site

security.title: Visible privacy and dependable operations from day one
security.deck: Trust does not come from an abstract technical promise. It comes from better screens, clearer roles, and continuity when the room is under pressure.

cards.title: Privacy-safe display
items: TV shows ticket, initials, and consult room
No full phone number or full patient name outside admin
Clear state for staff, patient, and room

cards.title: Lite roles, branding, and sites
items: Reception, admin, and management with separate access
Basic branding per clinic or small chain
Prepared for 1 to 5 sites without enterprise overhead

cards.title: Operational continuity
items: Offline outbox in kiosk
TV snapshot and visible reconnection state
Reception can keep operating and reprint tickets

items.question: Do we need proprietary hardware to launch?
items.answer: No. The pilot is designed to work with a browser, a TV or monitor, a PC or tablet, and a compatible thermal printer.

items.question: Does this replace the clinic's full medical software?
items.answer: Not at this stage. The suite competes as waiting room operations + booking + reminders + reporting, not as a full EMR.

items.question: Can we start with a single site?
items.answer: Yes. The first model is built for a pilot site, then expands to branding, lite multi-site, and dashboard layers only if the flow proves itself.

items.question: How do you reduce no-show if patients never touch the kiosk?
items.answer: The improvement does not depend on the kiosk alone. It comes from reminders, mobile check-in, queue visibility, and a front desk that sees the same state.

finalCta.title: If reception adopts it quickly, patients understand it, and management can measure it, the suite already has a buying story.
finalCta.deck: Start with daily operations, prove the patient flow, and close with the site dashboard when the budget conversation shows up.
